description: A PALS webbing-compatible magnetic holster designed to hold long firearms during breaching operations.

variants.description: A Kydex body holding four strong magnets inside.  MSRS stands for "magnetic shotgun retention system", as it was originally designed to securely hold breaching shotguns.  The "holster" can, of course, hold any weapon made of a ferromagnetic material.  <variant:black>

A Kydex body holding four strong magnets inside.  MSRS stands for "magnetic shotgun retention system", as it was originally designed to securely hold breaching shotguns.  The "holster" can, of course, hold any weapon made of a ferromagnetic material.  <variant:olive>

description: A nylon MOLLE pouch for collapsible batons.  Can hold most models of batons available on the market.  Typically worn by police officers who use webbing duty belts.

name.str: Blue Force Gear MOLLE baton pouch
name.str_pl: Blue Force Gear MOLLE baton pouches

description: A metal ring on a strap which attaches to the belt.  Can hold a PR-24 baton or a regular-sized flashlight.

variants.description: A metal ring with a tongue, held by a thin nylon strap.  Usually worn on the belt by an on-duty police officer, and holds the Monadnock PR-24 side-handle baton.  Can also hold standard-sized flashlight, though not smaller or larger ones.  <variant:black>

description: A nylon pouch for stun guns.  Can fit a variety of models.

variants.description: A nylon pouch for stun guns.  Fit for a specific model, but can fit similar models.  <variant:grey>

description: An arrangement of nylon straps locked together with a plastic buckle.  Can hold a medium- or large-sized breaching tool.  Straps to webbing, so could be carried on a plate carrier or a belt.

variants.description: A couple of tough nylon straps locked together with a plastic buckle.  Can hold a single breaching tool.  Straps to PALS webbing on your plate carrier, rig, or belt.  <variant:multicam>
